AMH(ng/mL): Anti-Müllerian hormone; often higher in PCOS-labeled participants in this cohort. Interpret with age and antral follicle count.
LH(mIU/mL): Luteinizing hormone; discuss with FSH and clinical hyperandrogenism. Rotterdam workups often note elevated LH or LH/FSH pattern.
FSH(mIU/mL): Follicle-stimulating hormone; compare with LH and AMH. Low FSH relative to LH can appear in PCOS phenotypes.
FSH/LH: Precomputed ratio in dataset; values >2 are often cited in PCOS discussions—verify against your lab’s reference.
BMI: Body mass index; PCOS-labeled group averaged higher than non-PCOS in this cohort, and higher than endometriosis-labeled cases in the harmonized comparison.
Age (yrs): Age in years; PCOS-labeled mean was younger than endometriosis-labeled mean in the harmonized comparison.
Follicle No. (R): Antral follicle count (right ovary) on imaging; among the strongest correlates with PCOS label in this cohort.
Follicle No. (L): Antral follicle count (left ovary); strongly associated with PCOS label in cohort models.
Cycle(R/I): Cycle regularity code (higher = more irregular in this coding). Irregular cycles overlap PCOS and other conditions.
Cycle length(days): Self-reported cycle length; shorter average in PCOS-labeled group in this dataset.
Endometrium (mm): Endometrial thickness on imaging; modest cohort difference—always interpret with cycle day and clinical context.
TSH (mIU/L): Thyroid-stimulating hormone; rule out thyroid-related cycle disturbance (not PCOS-specific).
PRL(ng/mL): Prolactin; elevated prolactin can affect cycles—consider if cycles irregular.
RBS(mg/dl): Random blood glucose; metabolic screening context for PCOS phenotypes.
Hb(g/dl): Hemoglobin; general health marker in cohort.
hair growth(Y/N): Clinical hyperandrogenism sign (hirsutism); more frequent in PCOS-labeled group.
Pimples(Y/N): Acne sign; associated with PCOS label in cohort chi-square tests.
Skin darkening (Y/N): Acanthosis nigricans–type sign; associated with insulin resistance and PCOS label.
Weight gain(Y/N): Reported weight gain; associated with PCOS label in this cohort.
